name: Ambiguous: delay exceeds cover + only alt has HIGH uncertainty (switch-vs-spot-vs-partial)
rubric_notes: Delay (120h) exceeds cover (96h) → Rule 2 fires immediate_switch canonical. BUT the only alternative has HIGH uncertainty — a cold-start supplier ChainPilot has no history with. Three actions are defensible: immediate_switch (no other option that meets timing; accept the supplier risk), partial_order (test the supplier with smaller volume; bridge with whatever stock remains), emergency_spot_buy (spot-market for the bridge while sourcing structurally — but the 25% premium on Newentrant is already at spot levels). GOLD customer at 72h is below the 120h delay window → material risk. Severity HIGH. Confidence MEDIUM or LOW depending on how aggressively the Arbiter surfaces the HIGH-uncertainty caveat. This scenario tests whether the system rightly distinguishes 'I have to act' from 'I trust this supplier.' Worth flagging that this scenario also informs the Uncertainty badge — the system should explicitly call out the cold-start status.
